Academic_App
Concept Design & Development Planning
Defined the purpose of building this app
Identified the core problem behind low assignment submission rate as task forgetting rather than lack of motivation
Analyzed current assignment sources including Gmail, Google Classroom, and verbal instructions
Defined the main goal as creating a system that does not rely on memory
Outlined initial core features for v1 such as manual task input, task list, deadlines, and completion tracking
Decided to adopt a step-by-step development approach using this chat as guidance
Chose Flutter as the primary frontend framework to prioritize mobile usability
Considered Firebase as the initial backend solution for simplicity and scalability
Defined a phased development plan from v1 to v5
Clarified the problem structure as an execution and memory issue rather than a motivation issue
Established a clear development direction focused on mobile-first application design
Defined a minimal viable product scope for v1
Created a structured development roadmap including v1 (basic task management) through v5 (external integration)
Set a practical development strategy of building one feature at a time
Final backend architecture (Firebase vs custom backend) not fully decided
No UI/UX design has been created yet
Integration with Gmail and Google Classroom remains complex and undefined
Set up Flutter development environment
Define task data structure (title, deadline, status, source)
Implement basic task list screen with mock data
Build task input screen for manual entry
Test basic task creation and display flow
Development will prioritize execution rate over feature completeness
Tasks must be structured as actionable units rather than abstract items
External triggers such as notifications will be essential in later stages
System will be developed iteratively from v1 to v5, increasing complexity gradually
